bead.title: FIX-01: author tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/seed/ (5 seed prompt files)
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>workflow-coverage | shared-fixture | runtime-parity | refusal-hardening</concerns>
<practices>tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/ is the single shared fixture across all 5 runtimes · Pass criteria are runtime-neutral; per-runtime variants vary by invocation/observability only · Static checks free, functional checks gated by TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1 · Genie scenarios may emit `genie:catalog-unreachable` skip until helix-96f7dd34 resolves · Symlink/inherit from tests/install/<runtime>/install.sh where reusable</practices>
<governing>FEAT-014 FIX-01 · TP-014 §4 fixture spec</governing>
</context-digest>

Create the five seed prompt files all scenarios consume:
- `seed/intent.txt` — ~30 words. Sparse user intent for scenario 1 (bootstrap). Example: 'Build a recipe-sharing app for amateur cooks. Users browse, save, and post recipes; basic comments and ratings; mobile-first.'
- `seed/change-request.txt` — ~40 words. SQLite → Postgres for scenario 3 (evolve).
- `seed/contradiction.txt` — ~50 words. Feature request that contradicts ADR-001 (e.g. MongoDB as primary store), for scenario 7b.
- `seed/ambiguous.txt` — ~15 words. Genuinely ambiguous intent for scenario 7c (e.g. 'Help me with that thing we discussed.').
- `seed/mechanical-prompts.txt` — list of one-line mechanical/non-HELIX prompts for scenario 7d non-match (e.g. 'pull and install', 'Use gpt-5.4-mini', 'Stop the worker.').
bead.acceptance: All five files exist with the documented sizes; each is plain text; no sensitive workspace identifiers.
